candidate_problems.text: Which interventions most effectively reduce bacterial evolutionary search throughput in real clinical and agricultural systems without imposing larger offsetting costs?
When do measures that reduce selection pressure, such as narrower use or combination therapy, merely shift resistance evolution into different ecological niches rather than slowing it overall?
How should human therapeutic design throughput be measured so that the claimed structural imbalance can be compared quantitatively across pathogens and treatment regimes?
